query: I want a skill that reviews a research paper for novelty — whether the contribution is actually new versus a known re-statement. Can you help me package my novelty-review approach as a walk-the-tree skill?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend packaging open-judgment novelty review as a walk-the-tree skill

Recommends a brainstorming-style, premortem-style, or framework-as-input skill instead (e.g., something patterned on running-adversarial-premortem or auditing-mathematical-claims) where judgment is the explicit deliverable rather than a leaf in a tree

Explains that walk-the-tree discipline only fits when the predicates are objectively checkable and the leaf actions are deterministic — novelty review is neither, because the predicate ('is this novel?') is the entire question
